svg.text: A manager forwards AI analysis into a decision process without checking the chain of reasoning because the output looks ready to use.
Start with the concrete case: A manager forwards AI analysis into a decision process without checking the chain of reasoning because the output looks ready to use.
Name the hidden structure: Trust calibration curve from undertrust to calibrated trust to overtrust.
Watch for the trap: Using convenience as a substitute for judgment.
Transfer the habit immediately: Write a team norm for when AI outputs require explicit review before action.

Unlike broad intro courses, this lesson turns the danger of overtrust into a practical operating skill through a real scenario, a visual model, a retrieval gate, and a transfer task built around AI trust calibration, real-world workflows, and verification discipline.
Using convenience as a substitute for judgment.

Write a team norm for when AI outputs require explicit review before action.
The goal is not to memorize the idea, but to build a repeatable habit for AI-assisted work. Ask the learner to produce a concrete artifact, not just an opinion.
